agents.name: agent_d
agents.description: Audits Revit schedules for missing parameter values and uses Claude to predict and fill missing data. Use this agent for any prompt about checking, auditing, filling, or populating schedule parameters (e.g. Fire Rating, Door Mark, room finishes).

actions.name: audit_data
actions.description: Check a schedule for missing parameter values; return statistics only (no writes).

actions.name: fill_data
actions.description: Predict and write missing parameter values via Claude. Modifies the Revit model.

actions.name: start_pipeline
actions.description: Run audit + fill + AI sanity check end-to-end and return combined results.

input_schema.schedule_name: string — exact name of the Revit ViewSchedule to operate on
input_schema.parameter_name: string — exact parameter name to audit/fill

agents.name: agent_i
agents.description: Cloud-hosted Revit-to-Notion BIM assistant. Holds a cloud copy of the Revit model uploaded via its own PyRevit button and uses Claude to answer natural-language questions about that data, push it to Notion databases, and diff snapshots to report what changed. Use this agent for any prompt about the cloud-uploaded model, summarising/querying elements (doors/walls/rooms etc.), exporting or pushing to Notion, or detecting changes between uploads.

actions.name: query_revit_data
actions.description: Natural-language question about the cloud-uploaded Revit data (counts, breakdowns by level/category, filtered samples). Read-only.

actions.name: push_to_notion
actions.description: Push filtered Revit elements to Notion — either as a brand-new database (mode='new_database') or as a versioned push to the fixed DB (mode='versioned'). Returns a job_id.

actions.name: compare_snapshots
actions.description: Diff the two most recently uploaded snapshots and report Added / Deleted / Modified elements. Set push_report=true to also push the diff to a new Notion database.

actions.name: check_job_status
actions.description: Poll a background Notion push job by job_id.

input_schema.category: string (optional) — filter by category before push
input_schema.keyword: string (optional) — substring match on element name
input_schema.push_report: bool (optional) — also push diff to Notion (compare_snapshots only)
